text: The surgeon marks the site for the feeding jejunostomy in the left paramedian position above the umbilicus using a FIMM port. Additional working ports and the camera port are positioned in the right flank.

The surgeon marks the site for the feeding jejunostomy using a 5mm trocar, located fifty centimeters from the DJ flexure. Diathermy is used to mark the site, and four transfacial fixation sutures are placed above, below, medially, and laterally to secure the jejunum to the parietal wall.

The surgeon uses a suture passer and needle holder to place the superior and lateral transfacial fixation sutures, lifting the jejunum with hitching sutures. A small enterotomy is created at the marked site for the feeding jejunostomy, and a purse-string suture is placed around the opening using 3-0 Vicryl sutures.

The surgeon introduces a twelfth French tube for the feeding jejunostomy through the previously marked site using a 5mm trocar. The tube is directed into the distal jejunal limb, extending approximately fifteen to twenty centimeters. The position is confirmed, and a pearl string suture is tied to secure the tube in place.

The surgeon uses a suture passer and needle holder to place medial and inferior hitching sutures from the jejunum to the parietal wall. An additional fixing suture is placed three centimeters distal to the feeding jejunostomy site to ensure a two-point fixation with a broad base. After securing the four hitching sutures around the feeding jejunostomy and the distal hitching suture, the neoperitoneum is partially deflated and the sutures are tightened.

Saline is instilled into the distal limb of the feeding jejunostomy to check its patency, confirming free flow. The completed feeding jejunostomy is observed with a broad base of excision to prevent torsion.
